cases.passage: It was a sunny Sunday morning, and my family was busy preparing for Mother's Day. My mother had been looking forward to this day for weeks, and I was excited to make it special for her. I had planned to surprise her with a bouquet of her favorite flowers and a handmade card. As I was getting ready, my younger brother kept asking me what we were doing, and I just smiled and told him that it was a surprise. My mother walked into the living room, and her eyes lit up when she saw the flowers and the card. She was overjoyed, and I felt happy knowing that I had made her day special. We spent the rest of the day together, cooking her favorite meal and watching her favorite movie.

questions.q: What was the occasion for the narrator's family to be busy on a Sunday morning?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the narrator's family was busy preparing for Mother's Day, indicating that it was the occasion.

questions.q: What did the narrator plan to surprise his mother with?
opts: (A) A bouquet of flowers and a homemade cake
(B) A bouquet of flowers and a handmade card
(C) A surprise party and a gift
(D) A family outing and a picnic
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the narrator had planned to surprise his mother with a bouquet of her favorite flowers and a handmade card.

questions.q: How did the narrator's mother react when she saw the surprise?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the mother's eyes lit up when she saw the flowers and the card, indicating that she was overjoyed.

questions.q: What did the narrator's family do together on Mother's Day?
opts: (A) They went for a walk and played games
(B) They cooked her favorite meal and watched her favorite movie
(C) They went shopping and had a picnic
(D) They played indoor games and watched TV
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that they spent the rest of the day together, cooking her favorite meal and watching her favorite movie.

questions.q: Why did the narrator feel happy on Mother's Day?
opts: (A) Because he got a new bike
(B) Because he made his mother's day special
(C) Because he got a surprise party
(D) Because he played games all day
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the narrator felt happy knowing that he had made his mother's day special.

cases.passage: I still remember the Mother's Day when I was in grade 5. My mother had been working hard to take care of our family, and I wanted to do something special for her. I spent the whole week thinking about what to get her, and finally, I decided to make her a handmade card and a gift. On the day of Mother's Day, I woke up early and made a special breakfast for her, and she was so touched by the effort. She told me how proud she was of me for being thoughtful and caring. That day was a memorable one for both of us, and it taught me the importance of showing appreciation for the people who care for us.

questions.q: What was the occasion for the narrator's special effort?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the narrator was thinking about what to get his mother on Mother's Day, indicating that it was the occasion.

questions.q: What did the narrator make for his mother?
opts: (A) A handmade card and a gift
(B) A handmade cake and a bouquet of flowers
(C) A surprise party and a gift
(D) A family outing and a picnic
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the narrator spent the whole week thinking about what to get his mother and finally decided to make her a handmade card and a gift.

questions.q: How did the narrator's mother react to the special breakfast?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the narrator's mother was touched by the effort he put into making a special breakfast for her.

questions.q: What did the narrator learn from the experience?
opts: (B) The importance of showing appreciation for the people who care for us
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the experience taught the narrator the importance of showing appreciation for the people who care for us.

questions.q: How did the experience affect the narrator's relationship with his mother?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that the experience was a memorable one for both the narrator and his mother, indicating that it strengthened their relationship.
